articles.title: Iran Makes White House ‘GO CRAZY’; Trump Shouts ‘WINNING’ For 1 Hour On Repeat In New Video
articles.description: A White House video showing U.S. President Donald Trump repeatedly saying “winning” has gone viral online after being posted as a looping clip. The one-hour repetition quickly sparked reactions across social media, with users debating the intent and message behind the unusual format. The clip adds to Trump’s ongoing messaging around “winning” the Iran war and his claims of progress on major policy fronts, even as critics question the optics and timing of the release amid rising global tensions and domestic debate.

articles.title: India successfully conducts 2nd test of long-range hypersonic anti-ship missile
articles.description: India has successfully tested its long-range hypersonic anti-ship missile (LR-AShM), boasting a strike range exceeding 1,500 km and speeds of Mach 10. This advanced weapon, capable of evading modern defenses and engaging large targets, significantly enhances India's maritime capabilities. The successful flight test marks a major stride in indigenous defense innovation, bolstering national security and self-reliance.

articles.title: Under a cloud: the growing resentment against the massive datacentres sprouting across Australian cities
articles.description: Residents say AI factories with unknown environmental impacts are being rushed into development as proponents argue Australia must ride the data boom or be left behind Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When West Footscray resident Sean Brown takes his 19-month-old boy to the park, their walk passes an imposing new building cheerily spruiked as “Australia’s largest hyperscale AI factory”, a datacentre called M3. He hates it: the construction noise from its constant expansion, the looming towers and the insistent background hum, the exhaust from the growing array of diesel generators that power the ranks of servers inside. articles.title: Switzerland’s Social Media Bus: The Art Experiment Turning Strangers Into Friends
articles.description: In Baden, Switzerland, a unique artistic project called “Line Zero” is transforming the idea of public transportation into a social experiment. Unlike traditional buses, this creative initiative is designed not just to move passengers, but to encourage conversation, connection, and shared experiences among strangers. Artists behind the project have reimagined the bus as a moving public space where social interaction becomes the real destination. Through interviews with artists, passengers, and the bus driver, this fascinating experiment explores how design and public art can challenge modern isolation and reshape human connection. As cities worldwide struggle with social disconnection, Switzerland’s Line Zero offers an innovative look at how transportation can become a platform for community building. Watch the full story behind this viral concept, the artistic vision, and why this unusual bus is capturing global attention. Could this be the future of public transport and social bonding?

articles.title: Alleged Realme 16T appears on Geekbench with key specs
articles.description: Realme launched the Realme 15T last September, and its successor, the 16T, is rumored to arrive soon. While there's no word from Realme about the 16T's launch yet, a Realme device bearing model code RMX5268 and rumored to be the Realme 16T, has appeared on Geekbench with its key specs. The RMX5268 scored 784 and 2,007 points in Geekbench's single and multi-core tests, respectively. It's listed in the benchmark database with Android 16 and 8GB RAM; however, a previous rumor claimed the 16T will also offer a 6GB RAM option. Realme 15T Powering the RMX5268 is MediaTek's MT6835...

articles.title: Received extremely severe alert on your phone: What is means and how this emergency notification system works
articles.description: India is testing its indigenous Cell Broadcasting system. This system sends emergency alerts to mobile phones during disasters. The test messages are being sent in English and Hindi. The system ensures quick dissemination of warnings for natural and man-made emergencies. It is operational across all states and union territories. Alerts are issued in over 19 Indian languages.
